message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Continue implementing the "Fábrica de Criativos v2" feature using Subagent-Driven Development (the recommended option chosen by the user)
   - The session started by completing the plan review loop (required by the writing-plans skill), fixing 12 issues in the implementation plan, then executing all tasks
   - The user explicitly chose "vai no recomendado, 1" for Subagent-Driven execution

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Subagent-Driven Development**: Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec compliance, then code quality) after each task
   - **Jake OS**: Flask SPA on port 5050, Neon/PostgreSQL (psycopg2 with RealDictCursor), Vanilla JS (IIFE pattern), CSS Glassmorphism
   - **Replicate REST API**: Image generation (sync with `Prefer: wait=60`), video generation (async — POST returns `prediction_id`, frontend polls `GET /status/<id>`)
   - **Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6**: Used for prompt expansion (5 specialist system prompts) and image style analysis (vision API)
   - **IIFE pattern**: Vanilla JS module pattern used across Jake OS frontend
   - **Fábrica de Criativos v2 Architecture**: Split View (left config panel / right result panel), 5 specialist prompt engineers, 11 Replicate models (5 image + 6 video), history with folders, async video polling

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`/root/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-21-fabrica-de-criativos.md`**
     - 11-task implementation plan, reviewed and approved after fixing 12 issues
     - Key fixes: route count (12→13), `_get_db()` and `_anthropic_client()` notes (both exist in app.py at lines 42 and 316), stub replacement anchors for Tasks 10/11, step numbering fix in Task 11, "Mover de pasta" JS handler, folder deletion UI, moved `import math as _math` and `import time as _t` to module level, Task 8 grep verification, skill commit note (outside repo)

   - **`~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md`** (Created, Task 1)
     - Global Claude Code skill with 5 specialist prompt engineers
     - NOT committed to git (outside the repo directory)
     - Frontmatter: `name: fabrica-de-criativos`, description starting with "Use when..."
     - 5 specialists: 🎯 Anúncios, 🎨 Conteúdo Criativo, 🌀 Psicodélico, 👤 Pessoas Realistas, 🌆 Cena/Ambiente
     - Each has: Quando usar, Estrutura canônica, Vocabulário, Evitar, Exemplo (simples → expandido)
     - Replicate model reference tables (5 image models, 6 video models)

   - **`/root/scripts/migrar_criativos.py`** (Created, Task 2, commit `1b3f4a0`)
     - One-shot migration creating `creative_folders` and `creative_history` tables
     - 3 indexes: idx_creative_history_tipo, idx_creative_history_folder, idx_creative_history_criado
     - Uses `core.db.get_conn()`, runs with `PYTHONPATH=/root /root/venv/bin/python3`

   - **`/root/jake_desktop/app.py`** (Modified across Tasks 3–6)
     - **Task 3** (commit `0bce465`, fix commit `3d819af`): Added constants block before `def _open_browser_delayed`:
       ```python
       import math as _math
       # (import time as _t was removed — time already at line 14)
       _CRIATIVOS_MODOS = {"anuncios", "criativo", "psicodelico", "pessoas", "cena"}
       _CRIATIVOS_MODELOS_IMAGEM = {5 models}
       _CRIATIVOS_MODELOS_VIDEO = {6 models with (slug, tipo) tuples}
       _CRIATIVOS_SYSTEM_PROMPTS = {5 specialist system prompts}
       _REPLICATE_BASE = "https://api.replicate.com/v1"
       def _replicate_headers(): ...
       ```
       Plus 3 routes: `POST /api/criativos/upload-imagem` (multipart, uploads to Replicate Files API, returns `{url, base64, mime_type, ok}`), `POST /api/criativos/expandir-prompt` (validates modo, calls Claude), `POST /api/criativos/analisar-referencia` (Claude vision, validates `modo_sugerido` with fallback to "criativo")
       Fix: added 10MB size limit before `arquivo.read()`
     - **Task 4** (commit `77e5737`): `POST /api/criativos/gerar-imagem` — uses `Prefer: wait=60`, fallback polling with `time.sleep(3)`
     - **Tasks 5+6** (commit `fab4273`, fix commit `cbc2b09`): 9 routes total:
       - `POST /api/criativos/gerar-video` — returns `{prediction_id, ok}` immediately
       - `GET /api/criativos/status/<prediction_id>` — polls Replicate, sanitizes prediction_id with regex
       - `GET/POST /api/criativos/pastas` — list and create folders
       - `DELETE /api/criativos/pastas/<pid>` — deletes folder, returns `criativos_desvinculados` count
       - `GET /api/criativos/historico` — paginated (page, limit, folder_id, tipo filters), uses `_math.ceil()`
       - `POST /api/criativos/historico` — saves item, validates required fields
       - `DELETE /api/criativos/historico/<hid>` — deletes item
       - `PATCH /api/criativos/historico/<hid>/pasta` — moves item to folder
       All DB routes fixed with `finally: conn.close()` and `conn.rollback()` on write exceptions

   - **`/root/jake_desktop/static/css/criativos.css`** (Created, Task 7, commit `27b06c2`)
     - Full glassmorphism CSS for split view
     - Classes: `.cri-layout`, `.cri-config`, `.cri-tipo-btn`, `.cri-modo-card`, `.cri-modelo-card`, `.cri-badge-tipo-t2v/i2v`, `.cri-btn-gerar`, `.cri-historico-grid`, `.cri-hist-card`, `.cri-paginacao`, `.hidden`
     - `@keyframes spin` for loading spinner

   - **`/root/jake_desktop/templates/dashboard.html`** (Modified, Task 8, commit `27b06c2`)
     - Replaced placeholder `<section class="page" id="page-criativos">` (old ~lines 689–839) with full split view HTML including: left config panel (toggle, mode cards, prompt, expanded panel, upload zone, model grid, generate button), right panel (tabs, result/preview, actions, history grid with filters+pagination), save-to-history modal
     - Added `<link rel="stylesheet" href="{{ url_for('static', filename='css/criativos.css') }}">` in `<head>` after anuncios.css
     - Added `<script src="{{ url_for('static', filename='js/criativos.js') }}"></script>` before `</body>` after anuncios.js
     - Key HTML IDs: `cri-tipo-imagem`, `cri-tipo-video`, `cri-modos`, `cri-modelos-grid`, `cri-btn-gerar`, `cri-painel-resultado`, `cri-painel-historico`, `cri-filtro-pasta`, `cri-btn-del-pasta`, `cri-modal-salvar`

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Plan review found 12 issues**: Route count mismatch (12→13), `_get_db()` undefined in plan context, `_anthropic_client()` undefined, no exact stub replacement anchor in Tasks 10/11, Task 11 step numbering skipped Step 4, "Mover de pasta" missing from JS, pasta deletion modal missing, `import math` inside route function, `import time as _t` inside route function, no CSS/JS tag verification, skill commit outside repo. All fixed before plan was approved.
   - **`import time as _t` duplicate**: Task 3 subagent added `import time as _t` but `time` was already imported at line 14. Fixed: removed the duplicate alias, updated Task 4 to use `time.sleep(3)` directly.
   - **No file size limit on upload**: Code quality reviewer flagged OOM risk. Fixed: added 10MB guard (`if len(file_bytes) > 10 * 1024 * 1024: return 413`) before `arquivo.read()` processes data.
   - **Connection leak in DB routes**: All 7 DB routes in Tasks 5+6 had `conn.close()` inside the try block. Fixed: moved `conn = _get_db()` outside try, `conn.close()` into `finally`, added `conn.rollback()` in except for write routes.
   - **ValueError on malformed query params**: `int(request.args.get("page", 1))` would crash on `?page=abc`. Fixed: wrapped page/limit/folder_id parsing in `try/except (ValueError, TypeError)` returning 400.
   - **prediction_id path traversal risk**: Unsanitized `prediction_id` forwarded to Replicate URL. Fixed: added `re.fullmatch(r'[a-zA-Z0-9]+', prediction_id)` check returning 400 if invalid.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Plan review loop**: Required by writing-plans skill. Two rounds — first found 12 issues, all fixed, second approved.
   - **Tasks 5+6 combined**: Implemented together in one subagent for efficiency since they're consecutive backend tasks on the same file.
   - **Tasks 7+8 combined**: CSS creation and HTML replacement done together in one subagent since they're tightly related (CSS classes referenced in HTML).
   - **`_anthropic_client()` and `_get_db()` context**: Added explicit notes in plan that these already exist in app.py (lines 316 and 42 respectively) so subagents don't redefine them.

6. All user messages:
   - "vai no recomendado, 1" — chose Subagent-Driven Development for plan execution

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Task 9**: JavaScript — criativos.js (layout, modos, modelos) — Create `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` with IIFE, state variables, toggle imagem/vídeo, mode selector, model grid with badges, and stubs for Tasks 10/11
   - **Task 10**: JavaScript — Upload, Prompt e Geração — Replace stubs `bindUpload`, `bindPromptActions`, `bindGerarBtn` with real implementation (upload via Replicate, expand prompt via Claude, generate image/video with polling)
   - **Task 11**: JavaScript — Resultado, Histórico e Pastas — Replace remaining stubs `bindResultadoAcoes`, `bindAbas`, `bindHistoricoEvents`, `carregarPastas`, `carregarHistorico` with full implementation including download, send to Subir Anúncios, save history, move to folder, delete folder with count confirmation

8. Current Work:
   Tasks 7 and 8 (CSS + HTML) were just completed and committed together as `27b06c2` ("feat: CSS + HTML da Fábrica de Criativos v2 — split view completo com histórico"). The implementer subagent reported DONE: CSS file created with full glassmorphism styles, old placeholder HTML replaced with split view HTML, both CSS/JS link tags inserted and verified (grep returned exactly 2 lines), Flask returned HTTP 200 on /login. Tasks 7 and 8 were in the process of being marked complete and Task 9 was about to begin.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Mark Tasks 7 and 8 as completed, then dispatch the implementer subagent for Task 9 (JavaScript — criativos.js layout, modos, modelos). The plan says Task 9 creates `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` as a new file with the complete IIFE boilerplate, state variables, `bindTipoToggle()`, `bindModos()`, `renderModelos()`, utility functions, MutationObserver for lazy init, and stub functions for Tasks 10/11. The exact stub old_string to preserve (per plan fix) is:
   ```javascript
     function bindUpload() {}
     function bindPromptActions() {}
     function bindGerarBtn() {}
     function bindResultadoAcoes() {}
     function bindAbas() {}
     function bindHistoricoEvents() {}
     function carregarPastas() {}
     function carregarHistorico() {}
   ```
   The HEAD_SHA before Task 9 is `27b06c2`.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /root/.claude/projects/-root/cf3d736a-f9d4-4db4-8829-2b4c6fa7cf54.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

# Fábrica de Criativos v2 — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Substituir a Fábrica de Criativos atual por versão completa com geração de imagem/vídeo (11 modelos Replicate), 5 especialistas de prompt via Claude, upload de referência, histórico paginado com pastas, e skill global do Claude Code.

**Architecture:** Backend Flask com 13 novas rotas em `app.py`; geração de vídeo assíncrona com polling via `GET /status/<prediction_id>`; frontend Split View (config esquerda / resultado direita) em IIFE Vanilla JS; banco Neon com 2 novas tabelas; skill Markdown com 5 engenheiros de prompt especializados.

**Tech Stack:** Python/Flask, psycopg2 (Neon PostgreSQL), Replicate REST API (sem SDK), Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6, Vanilla JS (IIFE), CSS Glassmorphism (padrão Jake OS)

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-21-fabrica-de-criativos-design.md`

---

## File Map

| Arquivo | Ação | Responsabilidade |
|---|---|---|
| `scripts/migrar_criativos.py` | Criar | Migration one-shot: tabelas + índices |
| `jake_desktop/app.py` | Modificar | 13 novas rotas `/api/criativos/*` (upload-imagem, expandir-prompt, analisar-referencia, gerar-imagem, gerar-video, status/\<id\>, historico GET, historico POST, historico/\<id\> DELETE, historico/\<id\>/pasta PATCH, pastas GET, pastas POST, pastas/\<id\> DELETE) |
| `jake_desktop/templates/dashboard.html` | Modificar | Substituir seção `page-criativos` (linhas 690–840) + link CSS/JS |
| `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` | Criar | IIFE completo: layout, modos, upload, geração, histórico |
| `jake_desktop/static/css/criativos.css` | Criar | Estilos da seção |
| `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md` | Criar | Skill global com 5 especialistas de prompt |

---

## Mapeamento de Modelos (referência para toda a implementação)

| Key (frontend/backend) | Slug Replicate | Tipo |
|---|---|---|
| `flux-1.1-pro` | `black-forest-labs/flux-1.1-pro` | imagem |
| `flux-dev` | `black-forest-labs/flux-dev` | imagem |
| `recraft-v3` | `recraft-ai/recraft-v3` | imagem |
| `ideogram-v3-turbo` | `ideogram-ai/ideogram-v3-turbo` | imagem |
| `imagen-4` | `google/imagen-4` | imagem |
| `wan-t2v-fast` | `wavespeedai/wan-2.2-t2v-480p` | video T2V |
| `wan-5b-fast` | `wavespeedai/wan-2.2-t2v-720p` | video T2V |
| `hailuo-02` | `minimax/hailuo-02` | video T2V |
| `seedance-lite` | `bytedance/seedance-1-lite` | video T2V |
| `runway-gen4` | `runwayml/gen4-turbo` | video T2V |
| `wan-i2v-fast` | `wavespeedai/wan-2.2-i2v-480p` | video I2V |

---

## Task 1: Claude Code Skill — 5 Especialistas de Prompt

**Files:**
- Create: `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md`

   375→- [ ] **Step 2: Adicionar rota de upload de imagem**
   376→
   377→Logo após as constantes acima:
   378→
   379→```python
   380→@app.route("/api/criativos/upload-imagem", methods=["POST"])
   381→@login_required
   382→def criativos_upload_imagem():
   383→    if "arquivo" not in request.files:
   384→        return jsonify({"error": "Campo 'arquivo' ausente"}), 400
   385→    arquivo = request.files["arquivo"]

def _replicate_headers():

```python
@app.route("/api/criativos/upload-imagem", methods=["POST"])
@login_required
def criativos_upload_imagem():
    if "arquivo" not in request.files:
        return jsonify({"error": "Campo 'arquivo' ausente"}), 400
    arquivo = request.files["arquivo"]
    file_bytes = arquivo.read()
    mime = arquivo.content_type or "image/jpeg"
    # base64 para análise via Claude
    b64 = base64.b64encode(file_bytes).decode("utf-8")
    # Upload para Replicate Files API para uso como URL em I2V
    try:
        headers = _replicate_headers()
        headers.pop("Content-Type")  # multipart não usa Content-Type JSON
        resp = requests.post(
            f"{_REPLICATE_BASE}/files",
            headers={"Authorization": headers["Authorization"]},
            files={"content": (arquivo.filename or "upload", file_bytes, mime)},
            timeout=30,
        )
        if not resp.ok:
            return jsonify({"error": f"Replicate upload: {resp.text[:200]}"}), 500
        url = resp.json().get("urls", {}).get("get") or resp.json().get("url", "")
        return jsonify({"url": url, "base64": b64, "mime_type": mime, "ok": True})
    except RuntimeError as e:
        return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
    except Exception as e:
        return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Adicionar rota de expandir prompt**

```python
@app.route("/api/criativos/expandir-prompt", methods=["POST"])
@login_required
def criativos_expandir_prompt():
    d = request.get_json() or {}
    prompt = (d.get("prompt") or "").strip()
    modo   = d.get("modo", "criativo")
    tipo   = d.get("tipo", "imagem")
    if not prompt:
        return jsonify({"error": "Campo 'prompt' obrigatório"}), 400
    if modo not in _CRIATIVOS_MODOS:
        return jsonify({"error": f"modo inválido. Válidos: {list(_CRIATIVOS_MODOS)}"}), 400

    client = _anthropic_client()
    if not client:
        return jsonify({"error": "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY não configurada"}), 500

    system = _CRIATIVOS_SYSTEM_PROMPTS[modo]
    tipo_hint = " Optimize for motion, camera movement, and temporal consistency." if tipo == "video" else ""
    try:
        msg = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
            max_tokens=200,
            system=system + tipo_hint,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Expand this prompt: {prompt}"}],
        )
        return jsonify({"prompt_expandido": msg.content[0].text.strip()})
    except Exception as e:
        return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
```

input.prompt:
You are a spec compliance reviewer. Review the implementation of Task 9 of the Fábrica de Criativos v2 plan against its spec.

## Spec for Task 9

Task 9 required creating `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` with:
- IIFE pattern `(function() { 'use strict'; ... })()`
- State variables: `_tipo`, `_modo`, `_modelo`, `_promptExp`, `_resultadoUrl`, `_uploadUrl`, `_uploadB64`, `_uploadMime`, `_usarI2V`, `_predId`, `_pollTimer`, `_histPage`, `_histFiltros`
- Model arrays: `_MODELOS_IMAGEM` (5 models) and `_MODELOS_VIDEO` (6 models including I2V)
- `init()` calling all bind functions and carregarPastas/carregarHistorico
- `bindTipoToggle()` — toggles between imagem/video, updates `_tipo` and `_modelo` defaults
- `bindModos()` — clicks `.cri-modo-card`, sets `_modo`, clears expanded panel
- `renderModelos()` — renders model cards with badges (tipo, vel, custo), handles disabled for I2V when `!_usarI2V`

No deviations found. The implementation matches the spec exactly.

content.text:
**Code Review — `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` (commit d6560fe)**

---

**VERDICT: APPROVED (with minor observations)**

No bugs, no security issues, and no memory leaks were found. Details below.

---

**1. Potential Bugs**

No bugs found.

- `_val`, `_set`, `_setText`: all guard against null with `if (el)` / ternary null checks before use. No null dereference risk.
- `renderModelos`: the grid null guard (`if (!grid) return`) is in place before any DOM manipulation.
- `_esc`: correctly calls `String(s)` before chaining `.replace()`, so it will not throw on non-string input (e.g. `null`, `undefined`).
- `bindTipoToggle`: `btnVid` is used inside the `btnImg` click handler (and vice versa) without null guards, but since both elements are peers in the same UI component, this is an acceptable assumption. It is not a bug in practice, though a defensive guard would be marginally safer.

---

**2. Security / XSS**

No XSS vulnerabilities found.

- All user-controlled or model-derived strings that go into `innerHTML` are processed correctly:
  - `m.nome` is escaped via `_esc()` (line 101).
  - `m.tipo`, `m.vel`, `m.custo` and `m.key` are assembled from the hardcoded `_MODELOS_IMAGEM` / `_MODELOS_VIDEO` arrays — no external input touches these values.
  - `data-key` attribute on rendered cards (line 100) is set to `m.key` from the same static array. No user input flows into that attribute.
- `_esc` handles the critical characters (`&`, `<`, `>`) correctly. Note it does not escape quotes (`"` / `'`), which would matter for attribute injection. This is not a risk here because no escaped value is placed inside a quoted HTML attribute; attribute values that come from the static arrays do not pass through `_esc` and are not attacker-controlled.

---

**3. Memory Leaks**

No memory leaks found.

- **`MutationObserver` (`_obs`)**: created once at module scope, observes `#page-criativos`. The observer is never disconnected, but since the page element is a long-lived SPA shell node (never removed from the DOM), this is the correct pattern. Disconnecting it after the first `active` detection would be a minor improvement but is not a leak in practice — and the `_iniciado` flag already prevents `init()` from running more than once.
- **`_pollTimer`**: declared at module scope (line 18). It is set but never cleared in this commit because `bindGerarBtn` and `bindResultadoAcoes` are stubs. No leak exists in the current code; the risk will need to be reviewed when those stubs are implemented (ensure `clearInterval`/`clearTimeout` is called on navigation away or on completion).
- **Event listeners in `renderModelos`**: the grid's `innerHTML` is fully replaced on each call, which removes all old DOM nodes and their associated listeners before new ones are attached. No accumulation occurs.
- **Event listeners in `bindModos` / `bindTipoToggle`**: these bind once to static DOM elements. No re-binding path exists, so no accumulation.

---

**4. Logic — Disabled I2V Cards**

Correctly implemented.

The selector on line 104 is:

```js
grid.querySelectorAll('.cri-modelo-card:not(.disabled)')
```

This precisely excludes cards that received the `disabled` class (applied when `m.tipo === 'I2V' && !_usarI2V`). Disabled I2V cards are rendered into the DOM for visual display but receive no `click` listener. The logic is correct.

---

**Summary Table**

| Category | Finding | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| Null dereference | None found | — |
| XSS | None found; `_esc` used correctly | — |
| Memory leaks | None in current code; `_pollTimer` cleanup deferred to stub implementations | Minor (future) |
| Disabled I2V click listeners | Correctly excluded via `:not(.disabled)` selector | — |
| `btnVid`/`btnImg` cross-reference without null guard | Acceptable assumption; both are peers in same component | Minor |

input.prompt:
You are reviewing Task 10 of the Fábrica de Criativos v2 implementation.

## Spec for Task 10

Task 10 required replacing 3 stub functions in `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` with real implementations:

**bindUpload():**
- Handles dragover/dragleave/drop events on `#cri-upload-zone`
- Handles file input change event on `#cri-upload-input`
- `#cri-btn-i2v` click: sets `_usarI2V = true`, switches to video mode if needed, sets `_modelo = 'wan-i2v-fast'`, calls `renderModelos()`
- `#cri-btn-analisar` click: calls `_analisarReferencia`
- `#cri-btn-remover-img` click: clears `_uploadUrl`, `_uploadB64`, `_usarI2V`, hides preview, resets input

**_processarUpload(file):**
- Uses FileReader to preview image (shows preview, hides placeholder)
- POSTs FormData to `/api/criativos/upload-imagem`
- Stores `_uploadUrl`, `_uploadB64`, `_uploadMime` from response

**_analisarReferencia():**
- Guards: `_uploadB64` must exist
- POSTs to `/api/criativos/analisar-referencia` with `{imagem_base64, mime_type}`
- Shows loading, clears on response
- Sets `_promptExp` from `data.prompt_sugerido`
- Sets `_modo` from `data.modo_sugerido`, updates mode cards
- Shows `#cri-expandido-painel`

**bindPromptActions():**
- `#cri-btn-regerar` click → `_expandirPrompt`

**_expandirPrompt(callback):**
- Gets prompt from `#cri-prompt`
- If prompt is empty or "(Analisado da referência)" and `_promptExp` exists, calls callback directly
- Otherwise POSTs to `/api/criativos/expandir-prompt` with `{prompt, modo, tipo}`
- On success: sets `_promptExp`, shows `#cri-expandido-painel`, calls callback if provided

**bindGerarBtn():**
- `#cri-btn-gerar` click: uses expanded prompt if available, else calls `_expandirPrompt` with `_gerar` as callback

**_gerar():** dispatches to `_gerarImagem()` or `_gerarVideo()` based on `_tipo`

**_gerarImagem():**
- POSTs to `/api/criativos/gerar-imagem` with `{prompt_expandido, modelo}`
- Shows loading at 40%, shows result on success

**_gerarVideo():**
- POSTs to `/api/criativos/gerar-video` with `{prompt_expandido, modelo, imagem_url?}`
- `imagem_url` included only when `_usarI2V && _uploadUrl`
- Gets `prediction_id`, starts `_pollVideo(0)`

**_pollVideo(tentativa):**
- GETs `/api/criativos/status/{_predId}`
- On `succeeded`: clears loading, shows result
- On `failed`: clears loading, alerts
- Otherwise: sets `_pollTimer = setTimeout(... 3000ms)` for next poll

**_setLoading / _clearLoading / _mostrarResultado:** utility display functions

**The 5 remaining stubs must still be empty:** `bindResultadoAcoes`, `bindAbas`, `bindHistoricoEvents`, `carregarPastas`, `carregarHistorico`

## Review

Read `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` and verify:
1. All functions above are present and implement the spec
2. Remaining 5 stubs are untouched (empty)
3. No XSS issues (check innerHTML usage — there is none in Task 10 code, all DOM manipulation is via `.src`, `.textContent`, `.value`, `.classList`)
4. `_pollTimer` assigned correctly for cleanup potential

Report: ✅ SPEC COMPLIANT or ❌ issues. Then: ✅ APPROVED or quality issues.

content.text:
You are reviewing Task 10 of the Fábrica de Criativos v2 implementation.

## Spec for Task 10

Task 10 required replacing 3 stub functions in `jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` with real implementations:

**bindUpload():**
- Handles dragover/dragleave/drop events on `#cri-upload-zone`
- Handles file input change event on `#cri-upload-input`
- `#cri-btn-i2v` click: sets `_usarI2V = true`, switches to video mode if needed, sets `_modelo = 'wan-i2v-fast'`, calls `renderModelos()`
- `#cri-btn-analisar` click: calls `_analisarReferencia`
- `#cri-btn-remover-img` click: clears `_uploadUrl`, `_uploadB64`, `_usarI2V`, hides preview, resets input

**_processarUpload(file):**
- Uses FileReader to preview image (shows preview, hides placeholder)
- POSTs FormData to `/api/criativos/upload-imagem`
- Stores `_uploadUrl`, `_uploadB64`, `_uploadMime` from response

**_analisarReferencia():**
- Guards: `_uploadB64` must exist
- POSTs to `/api/criativos/analisar-referencia` with `{imagem_base64, mime_type}`
- Shows loading, clears on response
- Sets `_promptExp` from `data.prompt_sugerido`
- Sets `_modo` from `data.modo_sugerido`, updates mode cards
- Shows `#cri-expandido-painel`

**bindPromptActions():**
- `#cri-btn-regerar` click → `_expandirPrompt`

**_expandirPrompt(callback):**
- Gets prompt from `#cri-prompt`
- If prompt is empty or "(Analisado da referência)" and `_promptExp` exists, calls callback directly
- Otherwise POSTs to `/api/criativos/expandir-prompt` with `{prompt, modo, tipo}`
- On success: sets `_promptExp`, shows `#cri-expandido-painel`, calls callback if provided

**bindGerarBtn():**
- `#cri-btn-gerar` click: uses expanded prompt if available, else calls `_expandirPrompt` with `_gerar` as callback

**_gerar():** dispatches to `_gerarImagem()` or `_gerarVideo()` based on `_tipo`

**_gerarImagem():**
- POSTs to `/api/criativos/gerar-imagem` with `{prompt_expandido, modelo}`
- Shows loading at 40%, shows result on success

**_gerarVideo():**
- POSTs to `/api/criativos/gerar-video` with `{prompt_expandido, modelo, imagem_url?}`
- `imagem_url` included only when `_usarI2V && _uploadUrl`
- Gets `prediction_id`, starts `_pollVideo(0)`

**_pollVideo(tentativa):**
- GETs `/api/criativos/status/{_predId}`
- On `succeeded`: clears loading, shows result
- On `failed`: clears loading, alerts
- Otherwise: sets `_pollTimer = setTimeout(... 3000ms)` for next poll

**_setLoading / _clearLoading / _mostrarResultado:** utility display functions

**The 5 remaining stubs must still be empty:** `bindResultadoAcoes`, `bindAbas`, `bindHistoricoEvents`, `carregarPastas`, `carregarHistorico`

## Review

Read `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` and verify:
1. All functions above are present and implement the spec
2. Remaining 5 stubs are untouched (empty)
3. No XSS issues (check innerHTML usage — there is none in Task 10 code, all DOM manipulation is via `.src`, `.textContent`, `.value`, `.classList`)
4. `_pollTimer` assigned correctly for cleanup potential

Report: ✅ SPEC COMPLIANT or ❌ issues. Then: ✅ APPROVED or quality issues.

content.text: Here is the full review of Task 11: --- ## Review: Task 11 — Fábrica de Criativos v2 File reviewed: `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` --- ### 1. bindResultadoAcoes() — COMPLIANT All 5 sub-bindings are present and correct: - `#cri-btn-download`: creates ``, sets `href = _resultadoUrl`, downloads as `.mp4` or `.webp` based on `_tipo`. Correct. - `#cri-btn-enviar-anuncios`: calls `window.JakeAnuncios.receberCriativo({url, tipo})` with proper guard, falls back to `alert`. Correct. - `#cri-btn-salvar-historico`: calls `_abrirModalSalvar`. Correct. - `#cri-modal-salvar-cancelar`: hides `#cri-modal-salvar` via `esconder()`. Correct. - `#cri-modal-salvar-confirmar`: calls `_confirmarSalvar`. Correct. --- ### 2. _abrirModalSalvar() — COMPLIANT Guards `_resultadoUrl` (returns early if falsy), calls `carregarPastasModal()`, then calls `mostrar('cri-modal-salvar')`. Correct. --- ### 3. carregarPastasModal() — COMPLIANT Fetches `/api/criativos/pastas`, populates `#cri-modal-pasta` with a default "Sem pasta" option followed by folder options. Folder names wrapped in `_esc()`. Correct. --- ### 4. _confirmarSalvar() — COMPLIANT POSTs to `/api/criativos/historico` with all required fields: `tipo`, `modo`, `modelo`, `prompt_original`, `prompt_expandido`, `url_resultado`, `folder_id`. Hides modal on success, calls `carregarHistorico()`. Correct. --- ### 5. bindAbas() — COMPLIANT `.cri-aba` click: removes `active` from all, adds to clicked. Shows `#cri-painel-resultado` or `#cri-painel-historico` based on `data-aba`. Calls `carregarHistorico()` on the historico tab. Correct. --- ### 6. carregarPastas() — COMPLIANT Fetches `/api/criativos/pastas`, populates `#cri-filtro-pasta` with "Todas as pastas" default + folders. Folder names wrapped in `_esc()`. Correct. --- ### 7. bindHistoricoEvents() — COMPLIANT - `#cri-filtro-pasta` change: updates `_histFiltros.folder_id`, resets `_histPage = 1`, calls `carregarHistorico()`. Correct. - `#cri-filtro-tipo` change: same pattern for `_histFiltros.tipo`. Correct. - `#cri-btn-nova-pasta`: prompts for name, POSTs to `/api/criativos/pastas`, refreshes `carregarPastas()` and `carregarHistorico()`. Correct. - `#cri-btn-del-pasta`: gets selected pasta from `#cri-filtro-pasta`, fetches count from `/api/criativos/historico?folder_id=X&limit=1`, shows confirm with count message, DELETEs `/api/criativos/pastas/`, resets `_histFiltros.folder_id`, refreshes. Correct. - `#cri-pag-anterior`: decrements `_histPage` only if `> 1`, reloads. Correct. - `#cri-pag-proximo`: increments `_histPage`, reloads. Correct. --- ### 8. carregarHistorico() — COMPLIANT - Builds query string with `_histPage`, `_histFiltros.folder_id`, `_histFiltros.tipo`. Correct. - Renders grid: video items get ``, downloads as `.mp4` or `.webp`. Correct. - Delete button: confirms, DELETEs `/api/criativos/historico/`, reloads. Correct. - Move-to-folder button: fetches pastas, prompts for selection via numbered list, PATCHes `/api/criativos/historico//pasta`. Correct. - Pagination: sets `cri-pag-info` text, disables `cri-pag-anterior` when `_histPage <= 1`, disables `cri-pag-proximo` when `_histPage >= pages`. Correct. --- ### XSS Safety Audit All `innerHTML` insertions that use user-sourced data apply `_esc()`: - Folder names in `carregarPastasModal()` and `carregarPastas()`: `_esc(p.nome)`. Safe. - Model name in `carregarHistorico()`: `_esc(item.modelo)`. Safe. - URL in `src` and `data-url` attributes in `carregarHistorico()`: `_esc(item.url_resultado)`. Safe. - Date (`data_fmt`) is derived from `.substring(0, 10)` of a date string — low risk, not escaped but only ISO date characters can appear. Acceptable. - Item `id` values inserted into `data-id` attributes are integers from the API — not user-sourced strings. Acceptable. - `nomePasta` in the delete confirm dialog is used in a `confirm()` call (not innerHTML), so no XSS risk there. --- ### Pagination Buttons Disabled Correctly - Anterior disabled: `_histPage <= 1`. Correct. - Proximo disabled: `_histPage >= pages`. Correct. - The anterior click handler also guards with `if (_histPage > 1)` before decrementing, providing a second layer. Correct. --- ### Broken References Check All called functions exist in scope: - `_abrirModalSalvar`, `carregarPastasModal`, `_confirmarSalvar`, `carregarHistorico`, `carregarPastas` — all defined in the same IIFE. - `mostrar`, `esconder`, `_val`, `_setText`, `_esc` — all utility helpers defined at lines 114-119. - No references to undefined functions found. --- ## Verdict ✅ SPEC COMPLIANT — All 5 required functions and their helpers are fully implemented, matching every requirement in the spec. ✅ APPROVED — XSS safety is correctly applied throughout, pagination disabling is correct, no broken function references exist.

input.prompt:
Review final da implementação completa da Fábrica de Criativos v2 no Jake OS.

## Spec original
`/root/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-21-fabrica-de-criativos-design.md`

## Plano de implementação
`/root/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-21-fabrica-de-criativos.md`

## Commits do branch (em ordem):
1. Task 1: `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md` (outside repo)
2. Task 2: `scripts/migrar_criativos.py` — commit `1b3f4a0`
3. Task 3: backend routes 1-3 (upload, expandir-prompt, analisar-referencia) — commit `0bce465` + fix `3d819af`
4. Task 4: backend route gerar-imagem — commit `77e5737`
5. Tasks 5+6: backend routes gerar-video, status, pastas CRUD, histórico CRUD — commit `fab4273` + fix `cbc2b09`
6. Tasks 7+8: CSS criativos.css + HTML page-criativos — commit `27b06c2`
7. Task 9: criativos.js base (IIFE, toggle, modos, modelos) — commit `d6560fe`
8. Task 10: criativos.js upload + prompt + geração — commit `e627b3b`
9. Task 11: criativos.js resultado + histórico + pastas — commit `07b021d`

## Files to review:
- `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` (592 lines)
- `/root/jake_desktop/static/css/criativos.css`
- `/root/scripts/migrar_criativos.py`

For app.py, only check the criativos-related routes (grep for "criativos" to find them).

## What to check:
1. All 13 backend routes are present in app.py
2. All spec requirements are met (5 specialist prompts, 11 models, async video, I2V gating, history pagination, folder deletion with count)
3. Security: SSRF risk in upload route (uploads to Replicate), SQL injection (parameterized queries), XSS (JS `_esc` usage)
4. The `_pollTimer` is set on video poll — verify it uses `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) so it stops naturally when video completes
5. Critérios de sucesso from the spec (listed at end of plan)

Report findings and overall verdict.

9. Task 11: criativos.js resultado + histórico + pastas — commit `07b021d`

## Files to review:
- `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js` (592 lines)
- `/root/jake_desktop/static/css/criativos.css`
- `/root/scripts/migrar_criativos.py`

For app.py, only check the criativos-related routes (grep for "criativos" to find them).

## What to check:
1. All 13 backend routes are present in app.py
2. All spec requirements are met (5 specialist prompts, 11 models, async video, I2V gating, history pagination, folder deletion with count)
3. Security: SSRF risk in upload route (uploads to Replicate), SQL injection (parameterized queries), XSS (JS `_esc` usage)
4. The `_pollTimer` is set on video poll — verify it uses `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) so it stops naturally when video completes
5. Critérios de sucesso from the spec (listed at end of plan)

Report findings and overall verdict.

1→# Fábrica de Criativos v2 — Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
     4→
     5→**Goal:** Substituir a Fábrica de Criativos atual por versão completa com geração de imagem/vídeo (11 modelos Replicate), 5 especialistas de prompt via Claude, upload de referência, histórico paginado com pastas, e skill global do Claude Code.

    46→## Task 1: Claude Code Skill — 5 Especialistas de Prompt
    47→
    48→**Files:**
    49→- Create: `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md`
    50→
    51→- [ ] **Step 1: Criar diretório e arquivo da skill**
    52→
    53→```bash
    54→mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos
    55→```
    56→
    57→Criar `~/.claude/skills/fabrica-de-criativos/SKILL.md`:
    58→
    59→```markdown
    60→---
    61→name: fabrica-de-criativos
    62→description: Use when generating AI images or videos for ad creatives, needing prompt engineering for Replicate models, or working on the Jake OS Fábrica de Criativos feature. Provides 5 specialized prompt engineers (Anúncios, Criativo, Psicodélico, Pessoas, Cena) and Replicate model reference.
    63→---
    64→
    65→# Fábrica de Criativos — Prompt Engineering Skill
    66→
    67→## Overview
    68→
    69→5 engenheiros de prompt especializados para geração de criativos com IA. Cada especialista expande um prompt simples em português para um prompt técnico profissional em inglês, otimizado para o tipo de criativo.
    70→
    71→**Regra universal:** Sempre gerar o prompt expandido em **inglês**. Manter o prompt expandido entre 50–120 palavras. Incluir sempre: sujeito principal, iluminação, estilo fotográfico/artístico, câmera/lente (quando aplicável), mood/atmosfera.
    72→
    73→---
    74→
    75→## Os 5 Especialistas
    76→
    77→### 🎯 Anúncios
    78→**Quando usar:** Criativos para Meta Ads, Google Ads, produtos, serviços, clínicas, negócios locais.
    79→
    80→**Estrutura canônica:**
    81→`[produto/serviço] advertisement, [sujeito + ação], [iluminação: studio/rim/softbox], [câmera: Canon/Sony 85mm f/1.4], commercial photography style, [background: clean/white/gradient], [color grading: warm/cool/neutral], [emoção: trust/confident/welcoming], Meta Ads format`
    82→
    83→**Vocabulário:** studio lighting, rim light, commercial photography, shallow depth of field, trust-inspiring, clean background, product hero shot, brand colors
    84→
    85→**Evitar:** artsy, experimental, surreal, cluttered backgrounds, dark moody tones
    86→
    87→**Exemplo:**
    88→- Simples: "clínica odontológica sorriso"
    89→- Expandido: "Professional dental clinic advertisement, confident woman smiling with perfect white teeth, soft studio lighting with rim light, shallow depth of field, Canon EOS R5 85mm f/1.4, commercial photography style, clean white background, warm color grading, trust-inspiring composition, Meta Ads format"
    90→
    91→---
    92→
    93→### 🎨 Conteúdo Criativo
    94→**Quando usar:** Arte conceitual, posts de redes sociais com apelo estético, storytelling visual, marcas de moda/lifestyle.
    95→
    96→**Estrutura canônica:**
    97→`[conceito] conceptual art, [sujeito + contexto], [composição: bold/dynamic/layered], [estilo: editorial/National Geographic/fine art], [iluminação: golden hour/dramatic/cinematic], [mood: narrative/emotional/striking], [técnica: high contrast/color grading]`
    98→
    99→**Vocabulário:** conceptual art, editorial style, bold composition, visual narrative, dynamic lighting, color contrast, fine art photography, storytelling
   100→
   101→**Evitar:** genérico, stock photo look, plain backgrounds sem intenção
   102→
   103→**Exemplo:**
   104→- Simples: "mulher e natureza"
   105→- Expandido: "Editorial conceptual art, woman merging with lush tropical nature, bold color contrast, dynamic composition, National Geographic style, golden hour lighting, environmental storytelling, high contrast shadows, award-winning photography"
   106→
   107→---
   108→
   109→### 🌀 Psicodélico
   110→**Quando usar:** Arte generativa, covers musicais, conteúdo alternativo, eventos, marcas disruptivas.
   111→
   112→**Estrutura canônica:**
   113→`psychedelic [tema], [geometria: fractal/kaleidoscope/spiral], [paleta: neon/vibrant/cosmic], [referência: DMT-inspired/surrealist/dreamscape], ultra-detailed, 8K resolution, [técnica: liquid geometry/morphing/infinite recursion]`
   114→
   115→**Vocabulário:** psychedelic, fractal geometry, neon colors, surrealist, kaleidoscope, liquid geometry, cosmic, DMT-inspired, interdimensional, crystalline
   116→
   117→**Evitar:** fotorrealismo, tons apagados, composição convencional
   118→
   119→**Exemplo:**
   120→- Simples: "portal dimensional"
   121→- Expandido: "Psychedelic dimensional portal, fractal geometry spiraling into infinite cosmos, neon cyan and magenta hues, liquid geometry morphing, DMT-inspired visuals, ultra-detailed, 8K resolution, surrealist dreamscape, kaleidoscope symmetry, cosmic interdimensional gateway"
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→### 👤 Pessoas Realistas
   126→**Quando usar:** Retratos, personas de cliente, modelos para anúncios, conteúdo com pessoas.
   127→
   128→**Estrutura canônica:**
   129→`Hyperrealistic portrait, [sujeito: idade/gênero/etnia/expressão], [iluminação: Rembrandt/natural window/golden hour], skin texture with subsurface scattering, [câmera: Canon EOS R5 85mm f/1.2], shallow depth of field, [contexto/roupa], [mood: candid/professional/authentic]`
   130→
   131→**Vocabulário:** hyperrealistic, skin texture, subsurface scattering, natural light, bokeh, candid, Rembrandt lighting, photojournalism, authentic expression, pore detail
   132→
   133→**Evitar:** AI-looking skin, plastic textures, symmetry perfeita demais, olhos artificiais
   134→
   135→**Exemplo:**
   136→- Simples: "homem de negócios confiante"
   137→- Expandido: "Hyperrealistic portrait, confident businessman mid-40s, natural window light with Rembrandt lighting, skin texture with subsurface scattering, Canon EOS R5 85mm f/1.2, shallow depth of field, professional business attire, authentic expression, photojournalism style, 8K resolution"
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→### 🌆 Cena/Ambiente
   142→**Quando usar:** Paisagens urbanas, natureza, arquitetura, cenários para fundo de anúncios.
   143→
   144→**Estrutura canônica:**
   145→`Cinematic [tipo de cena], [horário/luz: golden hour/blue hour/overcast], [técnica de composição: leading lines/rule of thirds/symmetry], [efeito: volumetric light/atmospheric haze/long exposure], wide angle [mm], [referência: award-winning landscape/National Geographic], [mood: epic/serene/dramatic]`
   146→
   147→**Vocabulário:** wide angle, leading lines, atmospheric perspective, golden hour, volumetric light, establishing shot, rule of thirds, long exposure, atmospheric haze
   148→
   149→**Evitar:** composições planas, luz plana sem drama, perspectiva de smartphone
   150→

2020→@app.route("/api/criativos/upload-imagem", methods=["POST"])
  2021→@login_required
  2022→def criativos_upload_imagem():
  2023→    if "arquivo" not in request.files:
  2024→        return jsonify({"error": "Campo 'arquivo' ausente"}), 400
  2025→    arquivo = request.files["arquivo"]
  2026→    file_bytes = arquivo.read()
  2027→    if len(file_bytes) > 10 * 1024 * 1024:  # 10 MB limit
  2028→        return jsonify({"error": "Arquivo muito grande. Limite: 10 MB"}), 413
  2029→    mime = arquivo.content_type or "image/jpeg"
  2030→    # base64 para análise via Claude
  2031→    b64 = base64.b64encode(file_bytes).decode("utf-8")
  2032→    # Upload para Replicate Files API para uso como URL em I2V
  2033→    try:
  2034→        headers = _replicate_headers()
  2035→        headers.pop("Content-Type")  # multipart não usa Content-Type JSON
  2036→        resp = requests.post(
  2037→            f"{_REPLICATE_BASE}/files",
  2038→            headers={"Authorization": headers["Authorization"]},
  2039→            files={"content": (arquivo.filename or "upload", file_bytes, mime)},
  2040→            timeout=30,
  2041→        )
  2042→        if not resp.ok:
  2043→            return jsonify({"error": f"Replicate upload: {resp.text[:200]}"}), 500
  2044→        url = resp.json().get("urls", {}).get("get") or resp.json().get("url", "")
  2045→        return jsonify({"url": url, "base64": b64, "mime_type": mime, "ok": True})
  2046→    except RuntimeError as e:
  2047→        return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
  2048→    except Exception as e:
  2049→        return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
  2050→
  2051→
  2052→@app.route("/api/criativos/expandir-prompt", methods=["POST"])
  2053→@login_required
  2054→def criativos_expandir_prompt():
  2055→    d = request.get_json() or {}
  2056→    prompt = (d.get("prompt") or "").strip()
  2057→    modo   = d.get("modo", "criativo")
  2058→    tipo   = d.get("tipo", "imagem")
  2059→    if not prompt:
  2060→        return jsonify({"error": "Campo 'prompt' obrigatório"}), 400
  2061→    if modo not in _CRIATIVOS_MODOS:
  2062→        return jsonify({"error": f"modo inválido. Válidos: {list(_CRIATIVOS_MODOS)}"}), 400
  2063→
  2064→    client = _anthropic_client()
  2065→    if not client:
  2066→        return jsonify({"error": "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY não configurada"}), 500
  2067→
  2068→    system = _CRIATIVOS_SYSTEM_PROMPTS[modo]
  2069→    tipo_hint = " Optimize for motion, camera movement, and temporal consistency." if tipo == "video" else ""
  2070→    try:
  2071→        msg = client.messages.create(
  2072→            model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
  2073→            max_tokens=200,
  2074→            system=system + tipo_hint,
  2075→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Expand this prompt: {prompt}"}],
  2076→        )
  2077→        return jsonify({"prompt_expandido": msg.content[0].text.strip()})
  2078→    except Exception as e:
  2079→        return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
  2080→
  2081→
  2082→@app.route("/api/criativos/analisar-referencia", methods=["POST"])
  2083→@login_required
  2084→def criativos_analisar_referencia():
  2085→    d = request.get_json() or {}
  2086→    b64  = d.get("imagem_base64", "")
  2087→    mime = d.get("mime_type", "image/jpeg")
  2088→    if not b64:
  2089→        return jsonify({"error": "imagem_base64 obrigatório"}), 400
  2090→
  2091→    client = _anthropic_client()
  2092→    if not client:
  2093→        return jsonify({"error": "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY não configurada"}), 500
  2094→
  2095→    system = (
  2096→        "You are an expert visual analyst and prompt engineer. "
  2097→        "Analyze the image and return ONLY valid JSON with two fields: "
  2098→        "'prompt_sugerido' (English prompt 50-120 words to recreate this visual style) and "
  2099→        "'modo_sugerido' (one of: anuncios, criativo, psicodelico, pessoas, cena). "
  2100→        "No markdown, no explanation, just the JSON object."
  2101→    )
  2102→    try:
  2103→        msg = client.messages.create(
  2104→            model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
  2105→            max_tokens=300,
  2106→            system=system,
  2107→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": [
  2108→                {"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": mime, "data": b64}},
  2109→                {"type": "text",  "text": "Analyze this image and return the JSON."},
  2110→            ]}],
  2111→        )
  2112→        raw = msg.content[0].text.strip()
  2113→        if "```" in raw:
  2114→            raw = raw.split("```")[1]
  2115→            if raw.startswith("json\n"):
  2116→                raw = raw[5:]
  2117→        result = json.loads(raw)
  2118→        # Validar modo_sugerido
  2119→        if result.get("modo_sugerido") not in _CRIATIVOS_MODOS:
  2120→            result["modo_sugerido"] = "criativo"
  2121→        return jsonify(result)
  2122→    except json.JSONDecodeError:
  2123→        return jsonify({"error": "IA retornou formato inválido"}), 500
  2124→    except Exception as e:
  2125→        return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
  2126→
  2127→
  2128→@app.route("/api/criativos/gerar-imagem", methods=["POST"])
  2129→@login_required
  2130→def criativos_gerar_imagem():
  2131→    d = request.get_json() or {}
  2132→    prompt  = (d.get("prompt_expandido") or "").strip()
  2133→    modelo  = d.get("modelo", "flux-1.1-pro")
  2134→    if not prompt:
  2135→        return jsonify({"error": "prompt_expandido obrigatório"}), 400
  2136→    if modelo not in _CRIATIVOS_MODELOS_IMAGEM:
  2137→        return jsonify({"error": f"modelo inválido. Válidos: {list(_CRIATIVOS_MODELOS_IMAGEM)}"}), 400
  2138→
  2139→    slug = _CRIATIVOS_MODELOS_IMAGEM[modelo]
  2140→    try:
  2141→        headers = _replicate_headers()
  2142→        headers["Prefer"] = "wait=60"
  2143→        resp = requests.post(
  2144→            f"{_REPLICATE_BASE}/models/{slug}/predictions",
  2145→            headers=headers,
  2146→            json={"input": {"prompt": prompt, "aspect_ratio": "4:5",
  2147→                            "output_format": "webp", "output_quality": 90}},
  2148→            timeout=90,

Now I have all the information needed for a thorough review. Let me compile the full report.

---

# Fábrica de Criativos v2 — Final Review Report

## What Was Done Well

The implementation is structurally sound and follows the project conventions faithfully. The IIFE pattern is consistent with Jake OS's other JS modules, the CSS glassmorphism tokens match the existing design system, the migration script is clean, and the backend uses parameterized queries throughout. The async video flow with `_pollTimer` is correctly implemented.

---

## 1. Route Audit — All 13 Routes Present

| Route | Method | Present |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/criativos/upload-imagem` | POST | YES (line 2020) |
| `/api/criativos/expandir-prompt` | POST | YES (line 2052) |
| `/api/criativos/analisar-referencia` | POST | YES (line 2082) |
| `/api/criativos/gerar-imagem` | POST | YES (line 2128) |
| `/api/criativos/gerar-video` | POST | YES (line 2176) |
| `/api/criativos/status/<prediction_id>` | GET | YES (line 2214) |
| `/api/criativos/historico` | GET | YES (line 2296) |
| `/api/criativos/historico` | POST | YES (line 2333) |
| `/api/criativos/historico/<id>` | DELETE | YES (line 2360) |
| `/api/criativos/historico/<id>/pasta` | PATCH | YES (line 2376) |
| `/api/criativos/pastas` | GET | YES (line 2242) |
| `/api/criativos/pastas` | POST | YES (line 2257) |
| `/api/criativos/pastas/<id>` | DELETE | YES (line 2278) |

All 13 routes are present. All carry `@login_required`. Verdict: PASS.

---

## 2. Spec Requirements Audit

**5 specialist prompts:** All 5 defined in `_CRIATIVOS_SYSTEM_PROMPTS` — `anuncios`, `criativo`, `psicodelico`, `pessoas`, `cena`. Vocabulary matches spec. PASS.

**11 models:** 5 image + 6 video, slugs match the spec table exactly. PASS.

**Async video with polling:** `gerar-video` returns `prediction_id` immediately without blocking. Frontend `_pollVideo` uses `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) — naturally self-limiting when status is `succeeded` or `failed`. PASS.

**I2V gating:** Backend validates `imagem_url` presence when `tipo == "i2v"` (line 2189–2190). Frontend disables the I2V card when `_usarI2V === false` at render time and only sends `imagem_url` when `_usarI2V && _uploadUrl` (line 297). PASS.

**History pagination:** `page` + `limit` params, ceiling division for `pages`, `total` count returned. PASS.

**Folder deletion with count:** Backend queries count before `DELETE`, returns `criativos_desvinculados`. Frontend fetches `historico?folder_id=X&limit=1`, reads `data.total`, shows it in the confirmation message. PASS.

**JakeAnuncios integration:** `window.JakeAnuncios.receberCriativo({ url, tipo })` called in `bindResultadoAcoes`. Graceful fallback to alert when the function is absent. PASS.

---

## 3. Security Review

### SSRF Risk in upload-imagem — IMPORTANT (should fix)

The upload route at `/root/jake_desktop/app.py` line 2036 sends `file_bytes` directly to the Replicate Files API. There is no validation of the MIME type beyond reading `arquivo.content_type`. A browser-controlled `Content-Type` header is not a reliable security boundary. An attacker can upload a file with a crafted `content_type` (e.g., `application/javascript`). However, because the bytes go to Replicate and not to your server's filesystem, the real SSRF vector is absent here — Replicate is a fixed, trusted destination hardcoded in `_REPLICATE_BASE`.

What remains is a weak input concern: the file's declared MIME type flows into `_uploadMime` (returned to the frontend), which then flows into the Claude `analisar-referencia` call as `media_type`. Claude's Anthropic API will reject unsupported media types with a 400, but the error message will be opaque. **Recommendation:** validate `mime` against an allowlist — `{"image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/webp", "image/gif"}` — and return a clean 415 response before the Replicate upload.

### SQL Injection — PASS

All queries use `%s` parameterization via psycopg2. The only dynamic SQL construction is in `criativos_listar_historico` where `where_sql` is assembled from hardcoded string literals, not from user input directly. PASS.

### XSS — PASS

The `_esc()` helper (`criativos.js` line 117) escapes `&`, `<`, and `>`. It is applied consistently to: model names in `renderModelos` (line 101), folder names in `carregarPastas` and `carregarPastasModal` (lines 401, 454), and history item model + date in `carregarHistorico` (lines 531, 536). URLs from Replicate CDN are also passed through `_esc` in history thumbnails (lines 530–531).

One minor gap: the `data-url` attribute in the history download button (line 539) uses `_esc(item.url_resultado)` which is correct for attribute context. However, the `img.src` and `vid.src` assignments in `_mostrarResultado` (lines 352, 355) use raw `_resultadoUrl` — a value that was returned from the backend's own Replicate API call. Since this URL is server-controlled (never injected from a user text field), the risk is negligible, but noting it for completeness.

---

## 4. _pollTimer / setTimeout Verification

`criativos.js` lines 324 and 327:

```javascript
_pollTimer = setTimeout(function () { _pollVideo(tentativa + 1); }, 3000);
```

This is `setTimeout`, not `setInterval`. Each call schedules exactly one future invocation. When `status === 'succeeded'` or `status === 'failed'`, no new `setTimeout` is scheduled and polling stops naturally. PASS.

The `_pollTimer` variable stores the timer handle, which means a future cancellation path (if needed) is possible. However, there is currently no cancel-on-navigate or cancel-on-new-generation logic — if a user clicks "Gerar" again while a video is still polling, `_predId` will be overwritten and two concurrent poll chains will run with one becoming orphaned. This is a **Suggestion** — acceptable for v2.

---

## 5. Migration Script

`/root/scripts/migrar_criativos.py` matches the spec schema exactly:
- `IF NOT EXISTS` guards make it idempotent and safe to re-run
- All three performance indices (`tipo`, `folder_id`, `criado_em DESC`) are created
- `ON DELETE SET NULL` on `folder_id` is correctly specified
- Uses `core.db.get_conn` consistent with the rest of the project

PASS.

---

## 6. CSS

The CSS file is complete (129 lines), covers all component states (`.active`, `.disabled`, `.dragover`, `.hidden`, `.loading`), and uses the Jake OS CSS variable tokens (`--ff-h`, `--ff-b`). The `@keyframes spin` animation is self-contained. PASS.

---

## Issues Summary

### Important (should fix before production)

**MIME type allowlist in upload-imagem**
File: `/root/jake_desktop/app.py`, line 2029.
Currently `mime = arquivo.content_type or "image/jpeg"` accepts any client-declared MIME type. Add a server-side check:

```python
ALLOWED_MIMES = {"image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/webp", "image/gif"}
if mime not in ALLOWED_MIMES:
    return jsonify({"error": "Tipo de arquivo não suportado"}), 415
```

This prevents the Claude API from receiving an unsupported `media_type` and provides a clean user-facing error.

### Suggestions (nice to have)

**Orphaned poll chains on re-generation**
File: `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js`, function `_gerarVideo` (line 294).
Before starting a new video generation, cancel any existing timer: `if (_pollTimer) { clearTimeout(_pollTimer); _pollTimer = null; }`. This ensures only one poll chain runs at a time.

**Pagination: next-page button not bounds-checked before fetch**
File: `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js`, line 511.
The "próximo" button increments `_histPage` unconditionally before the `carregarHistorico` response arrives. If the user clicks multiple times rapidly, the page counter can overshoot `pages`. Disable the button immediately on click and re-enable after the response. The `disabled` attribute is already set in `carregarHistorico` after data loads (line 588), so this is a minor race condition rather than a functional bug.

**Markdown stripping in analisar-referencia is fragile**
File: `/root/jake_desktop/app.py`, lines 2113–2116.
The code strips code fences by splitting on `"```"`, then checking for a `"json\n"` prefix. This handles the most common case but would fail on `\`\`\`JSON` (uppercase) or a fence with no language tag followed by whitespace. The system prompt instructs Claude to return plain JSON with no markdown, so this is a rare path — but a more robust strip using `re.sub(r'\`\`\`(?:json)?\s*', '', raw, flags=re.IGNORECASE)` would be safer.

---

## Critérios de Sucesso — Spec Checklist

| Critério | Status |
|---|---|
| Gerar imagem < 30s (exceto Imagen 4) | Architecture correct — `Prefer: wait=60` with 90s timeout; runtime depends on Replicate |
| Gerar vídeo T2V < 3 min | Async polling handles up to ~4 min at 85 attempts before no timeout; spec says 3 min — architecture sound |
| I2V só habilitado quando imagem uploadada | Frontend gating + backend validation — PASS |
| Histórico < 1s (paginação > 50 itens) | limit=20 default, max=50, three indices on the table — PASS |
| Análise de referência devolve prompt reproduzível | Claude-sonnet-4-6 with structured JSON response — qualitative, architecture sound |
| Integração com Subir Anúncios funcional | `window.JakeAnuncios.receberCriativo` call present with fallback — PASS |

---

## Overall Verdict

The implementation is **production-ready with one recommended fix** (MIME type allowlist). All 13 routes are implemented and decorated with `@login_required`. All 11 models and 5 specialist prompts match the spec. The async video flow is correctly implemented with `setTimeout`. SQL injection and XSS protections are in place. The migration script is idempotent and complete.

The MIME type validation gap is the only item that should be addressed before users can upload arbitrary files through the endpoint. The two suggestions (orphaned poll chains, pagination race) are low-priority quality-of-life improvements that do not affect core functionality.

The async video flow with `_pollTimer` is correctly implemented.

| `/api/criativos/historico` | POST | YES (line 2333) |
| `/api/criativos/historico/<id>` | DELETE | YES (line 2360) |
| `/api/criativos/historico/<id>/pasta` | PATCH | YES (line 2376) |

| `/api/criativos/pastas` | POST | YES (line 2257) |
| `/api/criativos/pastas/<id>` | DELETE | YES (line 2278) |

All 13 routes are present. All carry `@login_required`. Verdict: PASS.

**11 models:** 5 image + 6 video, slugs match the spec table exactly. PASS.

**Async video with polling:** `gerar-video` returns `prediction_id` immediately without blocking. Frontend `_pollVideo` uses `setTimeout` (not `setInterval`) — naturally self-limiting when status is `succeeded` or `failed`. PASS.

**I2V gating:** Backend validates `imagem_url` presence when `tipo == "i2v"` (line 2189–2190). Frontend disables the I2V card when `_usarI2V === false` at render time and only sends `imagem_url` when `_usarI2V && _uploadUrl` (line 297). PASS.

**History pagination:** `page` + `limit` params, ceiling division for `pages`, `total` count returned. PASS.

**Folder deletion with count:** Backend queries count before `DELETE`, returns `criativos_desvinculados`. Frontend fetches `historico?folder_id=X&limit=1`, reads `data.total`, shows it in the confirmation message. PASS.

**JakeAnuncios integration:** `window.JakeAnuncios.receberCriativo({ url, tipo })` called in `bindResultadoAcoes`. Graceful fallback to alert when the function is absent. PASS.

---

### SSRF Risk in upload-imagem — IMPORTANT (should fix)

The upload route at `/root/jake_desktop/app.py` line 2036 sends `file_bytes` directly to the Replicate Files API. There is no validation of the MIME type beyond reading `arquivo.content_type`. URLs from Replicate CDN are also passed through `_esc` in history thumbnails (lines 530–531).

One minor gap: the `data-url` attribute in the history download button (line 539) uses `_esc(item.url_resultado)` which is correct for attribute context. However, the `img.src` and `vid.src` assignments in `_mostrarResultado` (lines 352, 355) use raw `_resultadoUrl` — a value that was returned from the backend's own Replicate API call. Since this URL is server-controlled (never injected from a user text field), the risk is negligible, but noting it for completeness.

`criativos.js` lines 324 and 327:

```javascript
_pollTimer = setTimeout(function () { _pollVideo(tentativa + 1); }, 3000);

The `_pollTimer` variable stores the timer handle, which means a future cancellation path (if needed) is possible. This is a **Suggestion** — acceptable for v2.

## 5. Migration Script

`/root/scripts/migrar_criativos.py` matches the spec schema exactly:
- `IF NOT EXISTS` guards make it idempotent and safe to re-run

## 6. CSS

The CSS file is complete (129 lines), covers all component states (`.active`, `.disabled`, `.dragover`, `.hidden`, `.loading`), and uses the Jake OS CSS variable tokens (`--ff-h`, `--ff-b`). The `@keyframes spin` animation is self-contained. PASS.

### Important (should fix before production)

File: `/root/jake_desktop/app.py`, line 2029.
Currently `mime = arquivo.content_type or "image/jpeg"` accepts any client-declared MIME type. Add a server-side check:

**Pagination: next-page button not bounds-checked before fetch**
File: `/root/jake_desktop/static/js/criativos.js`, line 511.
The "próximo" button increments `_histPage` unconditionally before the `carregarHistorico` response arrives. If the user clicks multiple times rapidly, the page counter can overshoot `pages`. Disable the button immediately on click and re-enable after the response. The `disabled` attribute is already set in `carregarHistorico` after data loads (line 588), so this is a minor race condition rather than a functional bug.

File: `/root/jake_desktop/app.py`, lines 2113–2116.
The code strips code fences by splitting on `"```"`, then checking for a `"json\n"` prefix. This handles the most common case but would fail on `\`\`\`JSON` (uppercase) or a fence with no language tag followed by whitespace. ## Overall Verdict

The implementation is **production-ready with one recommended fix** (MIME type allowlist). All 13 routes are implemented and decorated with `@login_required`. message.content: ali so ta aparecendo pra colocar meu prompt simples, mas na hr de gerar vai gerar so com base no q eu falei ali?

content.text:
Não — quando você clica **Gerar**, o fluxo é automático:

1. Você digita o prompt simples
2. Clica **Gerar**
3. Claude expande automaticamente (aparece o painel "Original → Expandido" com o resultado)
4. Aí sim gera a imagem/vídeo com o prompt expandido

O painel expandido aparece **durante** o processo, não precisa clicar em nada extra. Se quiser ver/editar o prompt expandido antes de gerar, pode clicar no botão **"↺ Regerar"** que aparece no painel expandido separadamente.

Se já tinha um prompt expandido aberto e clica Gerar de novo, ele reutiliza o expandido existente sem chamar Claude de novo (economiza tempo).

message.content: me explica a diferença dos modelos que tem ali mesmo

content.text:
**Imagem:**

| Modelo | Melhor pra | Obs |
|---|---|---|
| **Flux 1.1 Pro** | Anúncios, retratos | Melhor custo-benefício geral, rápido |
| **Flux Dev** | Arte conceitual, qualidade alta | Mais detalhado, um pouco mais lento |
| **Recraft V3** | Design, UI, ícones | Especialista em vetor/estilo flat |
| **Ideogram V3 Turbo** | Texto dentro da imagem, logos | O melhor pra renderizar letras corretamente |
| **Imagen 4** | Fotorrealismo máximo | Mais caro, mais lento, resultado mais foto-real |

**Vídeo (T2V = texto vira vídeo):**

| Modelo | Resolução | Obs |
|---|---|---|
| **Wan T2V Fast** | 480p | Mais barato, mais rápido |
| **Wan 5B Fast** | 720p | Mesma família, mais qualidade |
| **Hailuo 02 Fast** | — | Ótimo movimento, estilo cinematográfico |
| **Seedance Lite** | — | ByteDance, bom equilíbrio qualidade/preço |
| **Runway Gen-4** | — | O mais caro, o mais premium do mercado |

**Vídeo I2V (imagem vira vídeo) — só aparece quando você faz upload:**

| Modelo | Obs |
|---|---|
| **Wan I2V Fast** | Anima a imagem que você subiu |

---

**Regra prática:**
- Anúncio de produto → **Flux 1.1 Pro** + modo 🎯 Anúncios
- Arte/criativo → **Flux Dev** + modo 🎨 Criativo
- Logo ou texto na imagem → **Ideogram**
- Vídeo rápido e barato → **Wan T2V Fast**
- Vídeo de qualidade → **Hailuo** ou **Runway Gen-4**

message.content: ali em imagem referencia,| usar como I2V E ANALISAR ESTILO OQ E

content.text:
**Usar como input I2V:**
Você sobe uma imagem e o modelo de vídeo **anima ela** — a imagem vira o primeiro frame do vídeo. Só funciona com o **Wan I2V Fast**. Útil quando você já tem uma foto do produto/pessoa e quer que ela "ganhe vida".

**Analisar estilo:**
Claude olha a imagem e **gera automaticamente um prompt** tentando recriar aquele estilo visual. Também sugere qual dos 5 modos combina mais (ex: detectou que é fotorrealista → sugere 👤 Pessoas). Daí você usa esse prompt gerado pra criar algo parecido com qualquer modelo.

---

**Na prática:**
- Tem foto do cliente e quer um vídeo dela → **I2V**
- Achou uma imagem com estilo que quer replicar → **Analisar estilo**

message.content: cara, quero conectar o obisidian aqui, em voce, para organizar todos esses projetos e pastaas, historicos, tudo, la no obisidian. acha isso valido e positivo? quais pontos positivos?

content.text:
Válido sim, faz bastante sentido. Pontos positivos reais:

**Organização visual que o Jake OS não tem**
- Graph view do Obsidian conecta projetos, clientes, campanhas — você vê as relações
- Pastas hierárquicas com links bidirecionais (ex: cliente → campanhas → criativos gerados)

**Histórico permanente e pesquisável**
- Tudo que o Jake gera (copies, criativos, relatórios) pode virar nota linkada
- Busca full-text rápida em tudo, sem depender de banco de dados

**Contexto entre sessões comigo**
- Você pode manter notas de briefing dos clientes, e eu leio antes de gerar qualquer coisa
- Menos repetição: "o cliente X prefere tom formal, azul, foco em conversão" fica documentado

**Planejamento de campanhas**
- Templates de campanha no Obsidian → preenche → eu executo
- Kanban nativo ou com plugin pra acompanhar status

**O que precisaria fazer pra integrar:**
- Via **MCP** (Model Context Protocol) — existe o `obsidian-mcp` que me dá acesso direto ao vault
- Ou mais simples: pasta do vault sincronizada e eu leio os `.md` direto

---

**Ponto de atenção:** O Obsidian fica local (ou no Obsidian Sync). Se a VPS não tiver acesso ao vault, a integração precisa ser via sync (iCloud, Dropbox, Git) ou MCP rodando na sua máquina local apontando pra mim na VPS.

content.content: Launching skill: superpowers:brainstorming

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /root/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.5/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec review loop** — dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent with precisely crafted review context (never your session history); fix issues and re-dispatch until approved (max 3 iterations, then surface to human)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec review loop" [shape=box];
    "Spec review passed?" [shape=diamond];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec review loop";
    "Spec review loop" -> "Spec review passed?";
    "Spec review passed?" -> "Spec review loop" [label="issues found,\nfix and re-dispatch"];
    "Spec review passed?" -> "User reviews spec?" [label="approved"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Review Loop:**
After writing the spec document:

1. Dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent (see spec-document-reviewer-prompt.md)
2. If Issues Found: fix, re-dispatch, repeat until Approved
3. If loop exceeds 3 iterations, surface to human for guidance

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`
Usando o skill de brainstorming para desenhar essa integração.
